name: How much money will I actually get every week while I'm off work?

mainEntity.name: How much money will I actually get every week while I'm off work?

acceptedAnswer.text: You will typically receive two-thirds (66.67%) of your average weekly wage, including overtime and bonuses, subject to state maximum caps. In Utah, you may also receive a small additional allowance for a spouse and dependent children.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Generally, your temporary total disability (TTD) benefits should be roughly two-thirds (66.67%) of your average weekly wage. This calculation is not just based on your base hourly rate; it must include overtime, bonuses, and even tips if they were reported. There are, however, state-mandated maximum and minimum caps that change annually. In Utah and Idaho, your 'Average Weekly Wage' is typically calculated using the 13 to 26 weeks of earnings prior to your injury.

Factors affecting the amount:
- Inclusion of overtime and performance bonuses.
- State-specific maximum benefit caps.
- Number of dependents (in some jurisdictions like Utah).

Double-check the insurance company's math, as they often 'forget' to include overtime or secondary jobs. You should provide copies of your paystubs from the six months prior to the accident to ensure the calculation is accurate. If the numbers seem low, an attorney can audit the insurer’s wage statement.
